channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Spring 2026 Student Course Evaluations Now Open
item.description: Date: Tuesday, April 14, 2026 (all day) - Spring 2026 course evaluations are now open. The evaluation period will be open from April 14th through April 30th. Your feedback is very important to the Institute, the five schools, and their departments, so please complete evaluation surveys for all your courses! Evaluations will be available through Thursday, April 30, 2026.
item.author: Students

item.title: Poster Review with the Success Center’s Writing Lab
item.description: If you’re preparing for the Undergraduate Research Symposium or an upcoming conference, join us for a Poster Review Pizza Night hosted by the Writing Lab! Bring your poster draft and enjoy pizza while receiving personalized feedback on your visual design, content clarity, and overall presentation. Tuesday, April 14th 5-6pm Success Center: Classroom 107, Folsom Library
item.author: Students

item.title: Mario Kart & Pizza Break with Lally
item.description: Take a break from classes and join students from across campus for an afternoon of Mario Kart, pizza, popcorn, and Lally swag in PITTS 5200. Whether you’re racing to win or just here for the pizza, it’s the perfect way to recharge and unwind as the semester wraps up. Wednesday, April 15 | 11:30 a.m.–2:00 p.m. No sign-up needed, just drop in and play!
item.author: Students

item.title: NYS Dept. of Transportation Info Session
item.description: Tuesday, April 14th 20265:30 pm - 8:00 pmHosted at the Center for Career and Professional Development - DCC 209 - Darrin Communications Center, Darrin Communications Center Troy, New York, United StatesHosted by the NYS Department of TransportationRegister here: https://rpi.joinhandshake.com/events/1869924/share_preview
item.author: Students

item.title: Rolling Applications Accepting Students for CSTEP!
item.description: The Collegiate Science & Technology Entry Program (CSTEP) is excited to announce that applications for the Spring semester are now open! CSTEP is an academic enrichment program designed to support New York State residents who are economically disadvantaged and pursuing careers in STEM or health-related fields.
item.author: Students

item.title: Starman: Screening & Filmmaker Q&A
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 29, 2026 4:30 PM - 7:00 PM - Join us for a screening of Starman, a New York Times Critics Pick. In this meditative, intergalactic biopic from Academy Award®-nominated director Robert Stone, we follow Gentry Lee, Chief Engineer for Planetary Exploration at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory, on his remarkable journey through space and on Earth. Followed by a live Q&A with Robert Stone, moderated by Dr. William Gibbons, Dean, School of Humanities, Arts and Social Sciences…
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI Debate Public Debate
item.description: The RPI Debate Team will be presenting a double-header public debate on Thursday, April 16 beginning at 6:30pm in Sage 3303. The first debate topic will be increasing nuclear energy production in the United States. The second debate topic will be censorship of social media. This event is open to all students, faculty, and staff.
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI Student Takes 1st Place at Columbia Business School’s Google Hackathon
item.description: In March, Lally undergrad Tazeem Mahashin ’28 (IT & Web Science / Finance) took first place at Columbia Business School’s Google Hackathon and also earned a Finalist award at HackPrinceton. Tazeem represented RPI with remarkable adaptability, demonstrating that knowing when to pivot is just as important as knowing how to build. Read the full article here.
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI Concert Choir and Wind Symphony Spring Concert
item.description: Join the RPI Concert Choir and the RPI Wind Symphony for their Spring Concert on Thursday, April 16th, at 7pm in the Chapel and Cultural Center at 2125 Burdett Avenue on RPI Campus. Admission is free and open to the whole RPI community.
item.author: Community

item.title: Physics, Applied Physics & Astronomy Colloquium: Dr. Joseph Falson; Department of Applied Physics and Materials Science ,Caltech
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 15, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - “Unconventional conventional superconductivity in two-dimensions”
item.author: Community

item.title: Monumental Death
item.description: April 16-May 8, 2026 How are we implicated in the images of power that surround us? How do our actions mimic what we have learned from those that lead us? Installation Kate Ladenheim
item.author: Community

item.title: ECSE Master Practitioner Seminar: “Research with the Power System in Mind: Linking Industry Needs to Academic Research to Enhance Best Practices”
item.description: Date: Thursday, April 16, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Join us for an ECSE Master Practitioner Seminar: “Research with the Power System in Mind: Linking Industry Needs to Academic Research to Enhance Best Practices” with Alex Shattuck, Director of Grid Transformation at Energy Systems Integration Group. Abstract and speaker bio can be found at the event URL.
item.author: Community

item.title: Nominate a Graduate Student for Student Spotlight!
item.description: Know a graduate student who is doing fascinating work or has an interesting story to tell? We’d like to highlight their accomplishments on our Student Spotlight page. Who embodies the BEST in your department? Who presents innovative and exciting ideas? Who leads others to become their best selves? Faculty, students, and staff may nominate a student here: https://surveys.rpi.edu/jfe/form/SV_1z9O3WGwx7ybE7c (Nominate a 2nd student by clicking the link again.) Questions? email madige@rpi.edu
item.author: Community

item.title: Center for Research Advancing Financial Technologies (CRAFT) Annual Conference
item.description: The Center for Research toward Advancing Financial Technologies (CRAFT) invites you to its annual conference meeting on Thursday, April 30, 8:00 a.m.–5:00 p.m. in the CBIS Auditorium. Registration is required; business attire is expected. CRAFT is an NSF-funded industry-university research center. The event features keynote talks and cutting-edge fintech research and practice presentations, hosted by RPI and Stevens Institute of Technology.
item.author: Community

item.title: Recognizing March Staff Promotions!
item.description: Congratulations to colleagues who received promotions this March! Your hard work, growth, and contributions continue to move RPI forward in meaningful ways. Log into Awardco to see who they are!
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: TIAA Confidential Counseling (In-Person)
item.description:
Date: Thursday, April 16, 2026 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM - A TIAA Financial Consultant will be available for one-on-one consultations on a broad range of retirement topics. Attend this upcoming event to help you become better informed and more confident about Rensselaer’s retirement plan benefits. Consultations will be conducted in 60 minute increments. To schedule your individual retirement counseling, please call TIAA at 1-800-732-8353 or online at https://www.tiaa.org/public/login/schedule-meeting

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
